## Skills
A skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.
### Available skills
- claude-code-subagent: Run Anthropic Claude Code CLI as a subagent for code tasks, validation, review, and implementation. Use when you want Opus or Sonnet execution with local tool use. (file: /Users/leemoore/.claude/skills/claude-code-subagent/SKILL.md)
- codex-subagent: Run OpenAI Codex CLI as a subagent for code tasks, review, validation, and implementation. Supports sync, async, parallel, and resume workflows with tiered output management. (file: /Users/leemoore/.claude/skills/codex-subagent/SKILL.md)
- copilot-subagent: Run GitHub Copilot CLI as a subagent for code tasks, validation, review, and implementation. Works with enterprise Copilot subscriptions. Multi-model access including Claude, GPT, and Gemini. (file: /Users/leemoore/.claude/skills/copilot-subagent/SKILL.md)
- cursor-subagent: Run Cursor CLI as a subagent for fast code tasks. Access to Composer 1 (very fast Sonnet-level) and GPT-5.2/Codex models with High/Extra-High reasoning. Focus on FAST variants for rapid iteration. (file: /Users/leemoore/.claude/skills/cursor-subagent/SKILL.md)
- docx: Comprehensive document creation, editing, and analysis with support for tracked changes, comments, formatting preservation, and text extraction. When Claude needs to work with professional documents (.docx files) for: (1) Creating new documents, (2) Modifying or editing content, (3) Working with tracked changes, (4) Adding comments, or any other document tasks (file: /Users/leemoore/.agents/skills/docx/SKILL.md)
- frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics. (file: /Users/leemoore/.agents/skills/frontend-design/SKILL.md)
- gh-address-comments: Help address review/issue comments on the open GitHub PR for the current branch using gh CLI; verify gh auth first and prompt the user to authenticate if not logged in. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/gh-address-comments/SKILL.md)
- gh-fix-ci: Use when a user asks to debug or fix failing GitHub PR checks that run in GitHub Actions; use `gh` to inspect checks and logs, summarize failure context, draft a fix plan, and implement only after explicit approval. Treat external providers (for example Buildkite) as out of scope and report only the details URL. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/gh-fix-ci/SKILL.md)
- imagegen: Use when the user asks to generate or edit images via the OpenAI Image API (for example: generate image, edit/inpaint/mask, background removal or replacement, transparent background, product shots, concept art, covers, or batch variants); run the bundled CLI (`scripts/image_gen.py`) and require `OPENAI_API_KEY` for live calls. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/imagegen/SKILL.md)
- ls-epic: Write complete, traceable Epics using Liminal Spec methodology. Covers User Profile, Flows, Acceptance Criteria, Test Conditions, Data Contracts, and Story Breakdown. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/ls-epic/SKILL.md)
- ls-impl: Implement stories using TDD discipline. Plan-mode compatible. Reads complete stories with functional and technical sections. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/ls-impl/SKILL.md)
- ls-research: Explore product direction and produce a PRD before feature scoping. Use when requirements are unclear, scope spans multiple features, or stakeholder alignment is needed. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/ls-research/SKILL.md)
- ls-story: Break an epic into functional stories with full acceptance criteria and test conditions. Validates coverage completeness and cross-story integration path coherence. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/ls-story/SKILL.md)
- ls-story-tech: Add technical implementation sections to functional stories. Tech Lead maps ACs to implementation targets, test strategies, and verification criteria. Validates via consumer gate. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/ls-story-tech/SKILL.md)
- ls-tech-design: Transform a Feature Spec into implementable Tech Design with architecture, interfaces, and test mapping. Validates the spec as downstream consumer. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/ls-tech-design/SKILL.md)
- lss-story: Write a single functional story with epic-quality rigor. Story-sized epic for focused changes that don't warrant the full pipeline. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/lss-story/SKILL.md)
- lss-tech: Perform inline technical design and embed results into a functional story. Story-sized tech design + enrichment with codebase analysis, architecture, interfaces, and test mapping. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/lss-tech/SKILL.md)
- mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK). (file: /Users/leemoore/.agents/skills/mcp-builder/SKILL.md)
- pdf: Comprehensive PDF manipulation toolkit for extracting text and tables, creating new PDFs, merging/splitting documents, and handling forms. When Claude needs to fill in a PDF form or programmatically process, generate, or analyze PDF documents at scale. (file: /Users/leemoore/.agents/skills/pdf/SKILL.md)
- pptx: Presentation creation, editing, and analysis. When Claude needs to work with presentations (.pptx files) for: (1) Creating new presentations, (2) Modifying or editing content, (3) Working with layouts, (4) Adding comments or speaker notes, or any other presentation tasks (file: /Users/leemoore/.agents/skills/pptx/SKILL.md)
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, update or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy. (file: /Users/leemoore/.claude/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md)
- webapp-testing: Toolkit for interacting with and testing local web applications using Playwright. Supports verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, capturing browser screenshots, and viewing browser logs. (file: /Users/leemoore/.agents/skills/webapp-testing/SKILL.md)
- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)
- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)
- slides: Build, edit, render, import, and export presentation decks with the preloaded @oai/artifact-tool JavaScript surface through the artifacts tool. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/.system/slides/SKILL.md)
- spreadsheets: Build, edit, recalculate, import, and export spreadsheet workbooks with the preloaded @oai/artifact-tool JavaScript surface through the artifacts tool. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/.system/spreadsheets/SKILL.md)
### How to use skills
- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.
- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.
- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.
- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):
  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.
  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.
  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.
  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.
  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.
- Coordination and sequencing:
  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.
  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.
- Context hygiene:
  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.
  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.
  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.
- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.

### How to use skills
- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.
- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.
- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.
- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):
  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.
  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.
  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.
  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.
  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.
- Coordination and sequencing:
  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.
  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.
- Context hygiene:
  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.
  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.
  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.
- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.

   107	    def __init__(

   110	        use_virustotal: bool = False,
   111	        virustotal_api_key: str | None = None,
   112	        virustotal_upload_files: bool = False,
   113	        policy: ScanPolicy | None = None,
   114	    ):
   115	        """
   116	        Initialize scanner with analyzers.
   117	
   118	        Args:
   119	            analyzers: List of analyzers to use. If None, uses default (static).
   120	            use_virustotal: Whether to enable VirusTotal binary scanning
   121	            virustotal_api_key: VirusTotal API key (required if use_virustotal=True)
   122	            virustotal_upload_files: If True, upload unknown files to VT. If False (default),
   123	                                    only check existing hashes
   124	            policy: Scan policy for org-specific allowlists and rule scoping.
   125	                If None, loads built-in defaults.
   126	        """
   127	        self.policy = policy or ScanPolicy.default()
   128	
   129	        if analyzers is None:
   130	            # Delegate to the centralised factory so core analyzer
   131	            # construction is defined in exactly one place.

   136

   139	                )
   140	                self.analyzers.append(vt_analyzer)
   141	        else:

   143	
   144	        # Warn if MetaAnalyzer is in the analyzers list -- it must be
   145	        # orchestrated separately via analyze_with_findings().
   146	        for a in self.analyzers:

   150	                    "produce findings via the normal analyze() pipeline. It will be "

   159	
   160	    def scan_skill(self, skill_directory: str | Path, *, lenient: bool = False) -> ScanResult:
   161	        """
   162	        Scan a single skill package.
   163	
   164	        Args:
   165	            skill_directory: Path to skill directory
   166	            lenient: Tolerate malformed YAML / missing fields in the skill.
   167	
   168	        Returns:
   169	            ScanResult with findings
   170	
   171	        Raises:
   172	            SkillLoadError: If skill cannot be loaded (when not lenient)
   173	        """
   174	        if not isinstance(skill_directory, Path):
   175	            skill_directory = Path(skill_directory)
   176	
   177	        skill = self.loader.load_skill(skill_directory, lenient=lenient)
   178	        return self._scan_single_skill(skill, skill_directory)
   179	
   180	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   181	    # Shared single-skill scanning logic (used by both scan_skill and
   182	    # scan_directory for identical behaviour).
   183	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

   185	    def _scan_single_skill(self, skill: Skill, skill_directory: Path) -> ScanResult:
   186	        """Run the full analysis pipeline on a loaded skill.
   187	
   188	        This is the shared implementation that both ``scan_skill`` and
   189	        ``scan_directory`` delegate to.  It guarantees identical two-phase
   190	        (non-LLM → LLM w/ enrichment) behaviour regardless of entry point.
   191	        """
   192	        start_time = time.time()
   193	
   194	        # Pre-processing: Extract archives and add extracted files to skill
   195	        extraction_result = self.content_extractor.extract_skill_archives(skill.files)
   196	        if extraction_result.extracted_files:
   197	            skill.files.extend(extraction_result.extracted_files)
   198	
   199	        try:

   202	            # Phase 2: LLM analyzers (enriched with Phase 1 context)
   203	            all_findings: list[Finding] = []

   215	                if analyzer.get_name() in ("llm_analyzer", "meta_analyzer"):

## Skills
A skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.
### Available skills
- claude-code-subagent: Run Anthropic Claude Code CLI as a subagent for code tasks, validation, review, and implementation. Use when you want Opus or Sonnet execution with local tool use. (file: /Users/leemoore/.claude/skills/claude-code-subagent/SKILL.md)
- codex-subagent: Run OpenAI Codex CLI as a subagent for code tasks, review, validation, and implementation. Supports sync, async, parallel, and resume workflows with tiered output management. (file: /Users/leemoore/.claude/skills/codex-subagent/SKILL.md)
- copilot-subagent: Run GitHub Copilot CLI as a subagent for code tasks, validation, review, and implementation. Works with enterprise Copilot subscriptions. Multi-model access including Claude, GPT, and Gemini. (file: /Users/leemoore/.claude/skills/copilot-subagent/SKILL.md)
- cursor-subagent: Run Cursor CLI as a subagent for fast code tasks. Access to Composer 1 (very fast Sonnet-level) and GPT-5.2/Codex models with High/Extra-High reasoning. Focus on FAST variants for rapid iteration. (file: /Users/leemoore/.claude/skills/cursor-subagent/SKILL.md)
- docx: Comprehensive document creation, editing, and analysis with support for tracked changes, comments, formatting preservation, and text extraction. When Claude needs to work with professional documents (.docx files) for: (1) Creating new documents, (2) Modifying or editing content, (3) Working with tracked changes, (4) Adding comments, or any other document tasks (file: /Users/leemoore/.agents/skills/docx/SKILL.md)
- frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics. (file: /Users/leemoore/.agents/skills/frontend-design/SKILL.md)
- gh-address-comments: Help address review/issue comments on the open GitHub PR for the current branch using gh CLI; verify gh auth first and prompt the user to authenticate if not logged in. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/gh-address-comments/SKILL.md)
- gh-fix-ci: Use when a user asks to debug or fix failing GitHub PR checks that run in GitHub Actions; use `gh` to inspect checks and logs, summarize failure context, draft a fix plan, and implement only after explicit approval. Treat external providers (for example Buildkite) as out of scope and report only the details URL. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/gh-fix-ci/SKILL.md)
- imagegen: Use when the user asks to generate or edit images via the OpenAI Image API (for example: generate image, edit/inpaint/mask, background removal or replacement, transparent background, product shots, concept art, covers, or batch variants); run the bundled CLI (`scripts/image_gen.py`) and require `OPENAI_API_KEY` for live calls. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/imagegen/SKILL.md)
- ls-epic: Write complete, traceable Epics using Liminal Spec methodology. Covers User Profile, Flows, Acceptance Criteria, Test Conditions, Data Contracts, and Story Breakdown. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/ls-epic/SKILL.md)
- ls-impl: Implement stories using TDD discipline. Plan-mode compatible. Reads complete stories with functional and technical sections. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/ls-impl/SKILL.md)
- ls-research: Explore product direction and produce a PRD before feature scoping. Use when requirements are unclear, scope spans multiple features, or stakeholder alignment is needed. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/ls-research/SKILL.md)
- ls-story: Break an epic into functional stories with full acceptance criteria and test conditions. Validates coverage completeness and cross-story integration path coherence. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/ls-story/SKILL.md)
- ls-story-tech: Add technical implementation sections to functional stories. Tech Lead maps ACs to implementation targets, test strategies, and verification criteria. Validates via consumer gate. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/ls-story-tech/SKILL.md)
- ls-tech-design: Transform a Feature Spec into implementable Tech Design with architecture, interfaces, and test mapping. Validates the spec as downstream consumer. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/ls-tech-design/SKILL.md)
- lss-story: Write a single functional story with epic-quality rigor. Story-sized epic for focused changes that don't warrant the full pipeline. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/lss-story/SKILL.md)
- lss-tech: Perform inline technical design and embed results into a functional story. Story-sized tech design + enrichment with codebase analysis, architecture, interfaces, and test mapping. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/lss-tech/SKILL.md)
- mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK). (file: /Users/leemoore/.agents/skills/mcp-builder/SKILL.md)
- pdf: Comprehensive PDF manipulation toolkit for extracting text and tables, creating new PDFs, merging/splitting documents, and handling forms. When Claude needs to fill in a PDF form or programmatically process, generate, or analyze PDF documents at scale. (file: /Users/leemoore/.agents/skills/pdf/SKILL.md)
- pptx: Presentation creation, editing, and analysis. When Claude needs to work with presentations (.pptx files) for: (1) Creating new presentations, (2) Modifying or editing content, (3) Working with layouts, (4) Adding comments or speaker notes, or any other presentation tasks (file: /Users/leemoore/.agents/skills/pptx/SKILL.md)
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, update or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy. (file: /Users/leemoore/.claude/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md)
- webapp-testing: Toolkit for interacting with and testing local web applications using Playwright. Supports verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, capturing browser screenshots, and viewing browser logs. (file: /Users/leemoore/.agents/skills/webapp-testing/SKILL.md)
- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)
- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)
- slides: Build, edit, render, import, and export presentation decks with the preloaded @oai/artifact-tool JavaScript surface through the artifacts tool. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/.system/slides/SKILL.md)
- spreadsheets: Build, edit, recalculate, import, and export spreadsheet workbooks with the preloaded @oai/artifact-tool JavaScript surface through the artifacts tool. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/.system/spreadsheets/SKILL.md)
### How to use skills
- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.
- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.
- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.
- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):
  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.
  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.
  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.
  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.
  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.
- Coordination and sequencing:
  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.
  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.
- Context hygiene:
  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.
  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.
  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.
- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.

- cursor-subagent: Run Cursor CLI as a subagent for fast code tasks. Access to Composer 1 (very fast Sonnet-level) and GPT-5.2/Codex models with High/Extra-High reasoning. Focus on FAST variants for rapid iteration. (file: /Users/leemoore/.claude/skills/cursor-subagent/SKILL.md)
- docx: Comprehensive document creation, editing, and analysis with support for tracked changes, comments, formatting preservation, and text extraction. When Claude needs to work with professional documents (.docx files) for: (1) Creating new documents, (2) Modifying or editing content, (3) Working with tracked changes, (4) Adding comments, or any other document tasks (file: /Users/leemoore/.agents/skills/docx/SKILL.md)
- frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics. (file: /Users/leemoore/.agents/skills/frontend-design/SKILL.md)
- gh-address-comments: Help address review/issue comments on the open GitHub PR for the current branch using gh CLI; verify gh auth first and prompt the user to authenticate if not logged in. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/gh-address-comments/SKILL.md)
- gh-fix-ci: Use when a user asks to debug or fix failing GitHub PR checks that run in GitHub Actions; use `gh` to inspect checks and logs, summarize failure context, draft a fix plan, and implement only after explicit approval. ## Skills
A skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.
### Available skills
- claude-code-subagent: Run Anthropic Claude Code CLI as a subagent for code tasks, validation, review, and implementation. Use when you want Opus or Sonnet execution with local tool use. (file: /Users/leemoore/.claude/skills/claude-code-subagent/SKILL.md)
- codex-subagent: Run OpenAI Codex CLI as a subagent for code tasks, review, validation, and implementation. Supports sync, async, parallel, and resume workflows with tiered output management. (file: /Users/leemoore/.claude/skills/codex-subagent/SKILL.md)
- copilot-subagent: Run GitHub Copilot CLI as a subagent for code tasks, validation, review, and implementation. Works with enterprise Copilot subscriptions. Multi-model access including Claude, GPT, and Gemini. (file: /Users/leemoore/.claude/skills/copilot-subagent/SKILL.md)
- cursor-subagent: Run Cursor CLI as a subagent for fast code tasks. Access to Composer 1 (very fast Sonnet-level) and GPT-5.2/Codex models with High/Extra-High reasoning. Focus on FAST variants for rapid iteration. (file: /Users/leemoore/.claude/skills/cursor-subagent/SKILL.md)
- docx: Comprehensive document creation, editing, and analysis with support for tracked changes, comments, formatting preservation, and text extraction. When Claude needs to work with professional documents (.docx files) for: (1) Creating new documents, (2) Modifying or editing content, (3) Working with tracked changes, (4) Adding comments, or any other document tasks (file: /Users/leemoore/.agents/skills/docx/SKILL.md)
- frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics. (file: /Users/leemoore/.agents/skills/frontend-design/SKILL.md)
- gh-address-comments: Help address review/issue comments on the open GitHub PR for the current branch using gh CLI; verify gh auth first and prompt the user to authenticate if not logged in. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/gh-address-comments/SKILL.md)
- gh-fix-ci: Use when a user asks to debug or fix failing GitHub PR checks that run in GitHub Actions; use `gh` to inspect checks and logs, summarize failure context, draft a fix plan, and implement only after explicit approval. Treat external providers (for example Buildkite) as out of scope and report only the details URL. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/gh-fix-ci/SKILL.md)
- imagegen: Use when the user asks to generate or edit images via the OpenAI Image API (for example: generate image, edit/inpaint/mask, background removal or replacement, transparent background, product shots, concept art, covers, or batch variants); run the bundled CLI (`scripts/image_gen.py`) and require `OPENAI_API_KEY` for live calls. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/imagegen/SKILL.md)
- ls-epic: Write complete, traceable Epics using Liminal Spec methodology. Covers User Profile, Flows, Acceptance Criteria, Test Conditions, Data Contracts, and Story Breakdown. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/ls-epic/SKILL.md)
- ls-impl: Implement stories using TDD discipline. Plan-mode compatible. Reads complete stories with functional and technical sections. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/ls-impl/SKILL.md)
- ls-research: Explore product direction and produce a PRD before feature scoping. Use when requirements are unclear, scope spans multiple features, or stakeholder alignment is needed. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/ls-research/SKILL.md)
- ls-story: Break an epic into functional stories with full acceptance criteria and test conditions. Validates coverage completeness and cross-story integration path coherence. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/ls-story/SKILL.md)
- ls-story-tech: Add technical implementation sections to functional stories. Tech Lead maps ACs to implementation targets, test strategies, and verification criteria. Validates via consumer gate. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/ls-story-tech/SKILL.md)
- ls-tech-design: Transform a Feature Spec into implementable Tech Design with architecture, interfaces, and test mapping. Validates the spec as downstream consumer. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/ls-tech-design/SKILL.md)
- lss-story: Write a single functional story with epic-quality rigor. Story-sized epic for focused changes that don't warrant the full pipeline. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/lss-story/SKILL.md)
- lss-tech: Perform inline technical design and embed results into a functional story. Story-sized tech design + enrichment with codebase analysis, architecture, interfaces, and test mapping. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/lss-tech/SKILL.md)
- mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK). (file: /Users/leemoore/.agents/skills/mcp-builder/SKILL.md)
- pdf: Comprehensive PDF manipulation toolkit for extracting text and tables, creating new PDFs, merging/splitting documents, and handling forms. - pptx: Presentation creation, editing, and analysis. When Claude needs to work with presentations (.pptx files) for: (1) Creating new presentations, (2) Modifying or editing content, (3) Working with layouts, (4) Adding comments or speaker notes, or any other presentation tasks (file: /Users/leemoore/.agents/skills/pptx/SKILL.md)
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. (file: /Users/leemoore/.claude/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md)

(file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)
- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)
- slides: Build, edit, render, import, and export presentation decks with the preloaded @oai/artifact-tool JavaScript surface through the artifacts tool. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/.system/slides/SKILL.md)
- spreadsheets: Build, edit, recalculate, import, and export spreadsheet workbooks with the preloaded @oai/artifact-tool JavaScript surface through the artifacts tool. (file: /Users/leemoore/.codex/skills/.system/spreadsheets/SKILL.md)
### How to use skills
- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.
- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.
- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.
- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):
  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.
  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.
  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.
  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.
  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.
- Coordination and sequencing:
  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.
  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.
- Context hygiene:
  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.
  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.
  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.
- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.

`bun run verify` was run first. On the current checkout it **fails** at format-check (not tests) due [test/core/scanner.test.ts:195](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/test/core/scanner.test.ts:195).  
`bun run test` passes: **49 files, 670 tests**.

**Findings (ordered by severity)**

- **P0: Alignment verification is not actually executed in TS behavioral analyzer (major parity break, false negatives).**  
  TS hardcodes `alignmentOrchestrator = null` and `_runAlignmentVerification()` is a no-op returning `[]`: [behavioral.ts:64](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/src/core/analyzers/behavioral.ts:64), [behavioral.ts:121](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/src/core/analyzers/behavioral.ts:121).  
  Python initializes and runs it when enabled + key is present: [behavioral_analyzer.py:103](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/skill-scanner/skill_scanner/core/analyzers/behavioral_analyzer.py:103), [behavioral_analyzer.py:206](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/skill-scanner/skill_scanner/core/analyzers/behavioral_analyzer.py:206).

- **P1: Provider parity gap in LLM stack (Bedrock/Vertex/Gemini direct unsupported in TS adapter).**  
  TS throws for these providers: [provider-config.ts:137](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/src/core/analyzers/llm/provider-config.ts:137), [provider-config.ts:141](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/src/core/analyzers/llm/provider-config.ts:141), [provider-config.ts:145](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/src/core/analyzers/llm/provider-config.ts:145).  
  Python supports these via config/runtime paths: [llm_provider_config.py:98](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/skill-scanner/skill_scanner/core/analyzers/llm_provider_config.py:98), [llm_provider_config.py:104](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/skill-scanner/skill_scanner/core/analyzers/llm_provider_config.py:104), [llm_provider_config.py:240](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/skill-scanner/skill_scanner/core/analyzers/llm_provider_config.py:240).

- **P1: Alignment default model conflicts with TS provider adapter.**  
  Alignment defaults to `gemini/gemini-2.0-flash`: [alignment-llm-client.ts:29](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/src/core/analyzers/alignment/alignment-llm-client.ts:29).  
  TS provider adapter rejects Gemini direct: [provider-config.ts:145](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/src/core/analyzers/llm/provider-config.ts:145).  
  This becomes an immediate runtime issue once behavioral alignment is wired.

- **P1: Sync API parity mismatch for cloud/LLM analyzers.**  
  TS `analyze()` throws and requires async usage: [llm/analyzer.ts:122](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/src/core/analyzers/llm/analyzer.ts:122), [aidefense.ts:163](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/src/core/analyzers/aidefense.ts:163), [virustotal.ts:148](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/src/core/analyzers/virustotal.ts:148).  
  Python provides sync wrappers returning findings: [llm_analyzer.py:278](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/skill-scanner/skill_scanner/core/analyzers/llm_analyzer.py:278), [aidefense_analyzer.py:227](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/skill-scanner/skill_scanner/core/analyzers/aidefense_analyzer.py:227).

- **P2: Silent error handling reduces diagnosability and can hide analyzer dropouts.**  
  TS factory swallows optional analyzer construction errors: [analyzer-factory.ts:96](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/src/core/analyzer-factory.ts:96), [analyzer-factory.ts:116](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/src/core/analyzer-factory.ts:116), [analyzer-factory.ts:132](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/src/core/analyzer-factory.ts:132), [analyzer-factory.ts:147](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/src/core/analyzer-factory.ts:147).  
  Python logs warnings with context: [analyzer_factory.py:122](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/skill-scanner/skill_scanner/core/analyzer_factory.py:122), [analyzer_factory.py:148](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/skill-scanner/skill_scanner/core/analyzer_factory.py:148).

- **P2: LLM message semantics differ (structured roles vs flattened prompt).**  
  TS flattens roles into one string: [request-handler.ts:108](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/src/core/analyzers/llm/request-handler.ts:108).  
  Python sends structured role messages to provider path: [llm_request_handler.py:200](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/skill-scanner/skill_scanner/core/analyzers/llm_request_handler.py:200).

**Parity assessment by module**

- `llm_analyzer / prompt_builder / response_parser`: mostly ported, but provider + request semantics differ.
- `meta_analyzer`: core behavior ported; TS scanner additionally applies meta findings inline (Python scanner warns meta is skipped in normal pipeline): [scanner.ts:338](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/src/core/scanner.ts:338), [scanner.py:147](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/skill-scanner/skill_scanner/core/scanner.py:147).
- `cross_skill_scanner`: strong parity.
- `behavioral/alignment/*`: files are ported and unit-tested, but behavioral integration is missing in TS.
- `virustotal`: now close parity including upload/poll flow.
- `aidefense`: close parity in async path; sync API differs.
- `scanner` and `analyzer_factory`: broadly aligned, but TS has weaker runtime diagnostics.

**Code quality**

- Type safety is acceptable but relies heavily on `unknown` + casting in analyzer internals.
- Async orchestration is good (`_runAnalyzer` prefers `analyzeAsync`): [scanner.ts:1056](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/src/core/scanner.ts:1056).
- Error handling is the main quality gap (silent catches).

**Test coverage parity**

Not all Python cases are ported 1:1. Coverage exists, but breadth is lower in key Phase 4 areas:

- LLM analyzer: Python 42 vs TS 9.
- Meta analyzer: Python 21 vs TS 13.
- Cross-skill: Python 17 vs TS 10.
- AIDefense: Python 46 vs TS 16.
- VirusTotal: Python has additional benign/upload integration scripts (`test_virustotal_benign.py`, `test_virustotal_upload.py`); TS has mocked upload/query coverage in [virustotal.test.ts](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/test/core/analyzers/virustotal.test.ts:273).
- Missing high-value TS test: behavioral alignment end-to-end (enabled + API key + finding emission).

**Phase 5 CLI readiness**

Mostly ready for a thin CLI over `SkillScanner`, but not clean yet due:

- Alignment path not operational in behavioral analyzer.
- Provider support mismatch for Bedrock/Vertex/Gemini.
- Config/factory surface is incomplete for some optional knobs (for example no explicit meta enable in config): [config.ts:30](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/src/types/config.ts:30), [analyzer-factory.ts:22](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/src/core/analyzer-factory.ts:22).
- `verify` currently failing due formatting.

**Recommendations**

1. **P0**: Implement behavioral alignment wiring in TS (`alignmentOrchestrator` init + real `_runAlignmentVerification` flow) and add an integration test for emitted alignment findings.
2. **P1**: Resolve provider parity (either implement Bedrock/Vertex/Gemini direct in TS provider adapter or explicitly gate with clear policy/docs and tests).
3. **P1**: Decide and document sync API contract. If parity is required, add sync wrappers; if async-only is intentional, enforce scanner-only usage and test for it.
4. **P2**: Replace empty catches with warning/error logs in factory/scanner/analyzers.
5. **P2**: Consider preserving structured message roles in TS LLM request path for closer model behavior parity.
6. **P2**: Fix formatting issue in [scanner.test.ts:195](/Users/leemoore/code/agent-cli-tools/skill-scanner-ts/test/core/scanner.test.ts:195) so `bun run verify` is green again.
